result: Contiguous-storage rewrite of JunctionTreeCholesky closes E9's ~5x headroom. Flat arenas + precomputed offsets + precomputed assembly map + reused scratch (no per-clique alloc, no position() search). SAME API, SAME exact answer (4 tests + byte-identical examples). MF solve 2.6-2.8x faster (d=889: 31.7us vs 85us); measured speedup vs dense 5.9-255x -> 16.7-682x; flop/measured gap 5.0x -> 1.86x.

novelty_classification.note: Resolves F-EVO-11's open 'contiguous-storage rewrite' item; confirms E9's diagnosis (allocation overhead) was correct. In-place component update (no v2/new type, §6.5 #13).

components_added_or_updated: JunctionTreeCholesky internals -> flat-arena contiguous storage (public API unchanged); note + F-EVO-11 limitation updated
framework_impact.open_question_resolved: F-EVO-11 'contiguous-storage multifrontal -> close the ~5x gap' -> DONE: 5x -> 1.86x, MF solve 2.7x faster

source_changes: src/junction_tree.rs (contiguous rewrite + buffer-based *_into helpers + dead n_sep removed + test helper flat-layout update)
regression_tests_added: (no NEW tests -- the 4 existing exactness/locality tests guard the rewrite; correctness confirmed by == dense + byte-identical E5-E8 example outputs)
honest_scope: 682x is vs a Gaussian dense baseline (~2x flops of pure Cholesky) -> layout-independent number is the 2.7x self-speedup; Cholesky baseline would show ~2x more residual
remaining headroom (blocked/SIMD triangular kernels) NOT pursued -- E9 caveat closed
rewrite changed only memory layout, arithmetic identical (guarded by exactness tests)
anti_pattern_check: no §6.5 anti-patterns: in-place component update (NOT JunctionTreeCholesky2/_contiguous, #13); one implementation of the linear-algebra helpers (buffer-based *_into, reused by solve + solve_block_diagonal, no duplication).
environment_note: Run on the Mac; kato15 NOT synced -- origin main ahead, fast-forwards on next pull.

next_experiment_proposal.name: blocked/SIMD triangular kernels + dense-Cholesky baseline (residual below ceiling); optimized refactorize_path incremental solver; higher-order (degree>=3) representability
